reason_for_request: 根据 unresolved cluster 请求 LLM 给出注释链接修补建议

request_prompt.system: 你是 FNM 注释修补助手（同时处理 endnote 和 footnote 两种 note_system）。只处理已经确认的 unresolved cluster，不要改 section、note zone、标题或原文。优先依据页面截图判断，不要被 OCR 坏掉的数字误导。如果截图已经足够清楚，就不要退回 needs_review。你只能输出 JSON 数组；每项 action 只能是 match、ignore_ref、synthesize_anchor、synthesize_note_item 或 needs_review。match 需要 note_item_id、anchor_id、confidence、reason；ignore_ref 需要 anchor_id、confidence、reason；synthesize_anchor 仅在正文里能找到一个独一无二的短语锚点、但该锚点没有对应的结构化 anchor 记录时使用，需要 note_item_id、anchor_phrase（从正文中原样抄写的 3~12 词唯一短语，不要自编）、confidence、reason；synthesize_note_item 仅在截图里能清楚看到同页注释文本、但 OCR / 结构化流程完全没产出 note item 时使用，需要 anchor_id、marker、note_text、confidence、reason；若某条 note 当前只是错误地绑到了 synthetic / 跨页锚点，而截图清楚显示它应改绑到当前显式锚点，也直接用 match，不要 needs_review。needs_review 需要 reason。

unmatched_note_items.source_text: Shen Kuo's Lingyuan fang 靈苑方 (1048/77): “Cinnabar powder” 辰砂散 $ ^{1} $
A case record by Sun Zhao 孫兆 (late 11 $ ^{th} $ century; see 3.7, pp. 65-65) [Sun 孫] $ ^{2} $

Xu Shuwei's 許叔微 Puji benshi fang 普濟本事方 (1132): recipes for "Will-appeasing paste" (寧志膏) and "Fright-Qi pill" (驚氣丸), as well as three case files that Xu appended to his entry on the latter [Ben 本, from the title of Xu's book] $ ^{3} $

Chen Yan's 陳言 Sanyin fang 三因方 (1174): three recipes, "Guo-alum elixir" (號礬丹), $ ^{4} $ the "Heart-pacifying elixir" (鎮心丹), and a modified version of the "Lesser life-prolonging decoction" (小續命湯) [Wu 無, from Chen's $ \ddot{\gamma} $ Wuze 無擇] $ ^{5} $

Zhang Congzheng’s 張從正 Rumen shiqin 儒門事親 (ca.
: four case records [Zihe 子和, Zhang’s $ z_{i} $]

Wang Haogu's 王好古 Yinzheng lieli 陰證略例 (author's prefaces from 1232 and 1243): one case record and a commentary on some passages from the Inner Canon concerning madness [Hai 海, short for Wang's bao Haicang 海藏] $ ^{8} $

page_contexts.ocr_excerpt: ## Appendix 3 # The madness section of Lou Ying's Yixue gangmu 醫學綱目 (1396) In section 5.2, I cited Lou Ying's 櫻英 (1320-1400) treatise as an example of intertextual composition and as good representatives of the zhèzhong (5.1) approach to the textual past that consisted in selectively borrowing approaches from previous books and authors. Here is a list of the works cited in the madness section of Lou Ying's Yixue gangmu. Lou referred to each book or author by one or two distinctive characters, which I cite in square brackets at the end of each entry. 1. The Suwen 素問 (Basic Questions): short passages from pian 28, 46, 74, and 79, the latter along with Wang Bing's 王冰 eighth-century annotations [cited as Su 素] 2. The Lingshu 靈樞 (Divine Pivot): pian 22 and 78 [Ling 靈] 3. The Nanjing 難經 (Canon of Problems): problems 20 and 59 [Nan 難] 4. The Zhenjiu jiayi jing 針灸甲乙經 (AB Canon of Acumoxa) [Jia 甲], compiled by Huangfu Mi in the third century CE. 5. Zhang Ji 張機: “Fangji and foxglove decoction” 防已地黃湯 from an entry called “Fangji and foxglove decoction” (防已地黃湯方) in Jingui yaolüe 金匱要略, juan 1, section 5 [Zhong 仲, from Zhang Ji’s zi Zhongjing 仲景]

6. Shen Kuo's Lingyuan fang 靈苑方 (1048/77): “Cinnabar powder” 辰砂散 $ ^{1} $ 7. A case record by Sun Zhao 孫兆 (late 11 $ ^{th} $ century; see 3.7, pp. 65-65) [Sun 孫] $ ^{2} $ 8. Xu Shuwei's 許叔微 Puji benshi fang 普濟本事方 (1132): recipes for "Will-appeasing paste" (寧志膏) and "Fright-Qi pill" (驚氣丸), as well as three case files that Xu appended to his entry on the latter [Ben 本, from the title of Xu's book] $ ^{3} $ 9. Chen Yan's 陳言 Sanyin fang 三因方 (1174): three recipes, "Guo-alum elixir" (號礬丹), $ ^{4} $ the "Heart-pacifying elixir" (鎮心丹), and a modified version of the "Lesser life-prolonging decoction" (小續命湯) [Wu 無, from Chen's $ \ddot{\gamma} $ Wuze 無擇] $ ^{5} $ 10. Liu Wansu's 劉完素 Suwen bingji qiyi baoming ji 素問病機氣宜保命集 (1186): for the “Angelica Qi-supporting decoction” (當歸承氣湯) [Bao 保] $ ^{6} $ 11. Zhang Congzheng’s 張從正 Rumen shiqin 儒門事親 (ca. 1228): four case records [Zihe 子和, Zhang’s $ z_{i} $] 12. Wang Haogu's 王好古 Yinzheng lieli 陰證略例 (author's prefaces from 1232 and 1243): one case record and a commentary on some passages from the Inner Canon concerning madness [Hai 海, short for Wang's bao Haicang 海藏] $ ^{8} $

13. Shatumusu's 沙圖穆蘇 Ruizhu tang jingyan fang 瑞竹堂經驗方 (1323): one recipe, "Coming-to paste" (Laisu gao 來蘇膏), designed to induce vomiting in cases of Wind strokes [Zhu'竹] $ ^{9} $ 14. Wei Yilin's 危亦林 Shiyi dexiao fang 世醫得效方 (1337; first printed in 1345): three recipes (“Instant-drunkenness paste” 一醉膏, $ ^{10} $ “Turmeric and alum powder” 鬱謦散, $ ^{11} $ and “Shrubby sophora decoction” 苦參湯 $ ^{12} $ and one case $ ^{13} $ [Shi 世]

## Appendix 4 ## Ming editions of books by Liu Chun 劉純 (1340-1412) Liu Chun's 劉純 medical treatises were widely read in the fifteenth century and thus contributed to the diffusion of Danxi medicine, the doctrines attributed to Zhu Zhenheng 朱震亨 (1282-1358) that became so central to medical thinking in the fifteenth and sixteenth centuries. $ ^{1} $ This appendix lists most known editions of four works by Liu Chun: Yijing xiaoxue 醫經小學 [“Elementary Learning on the Medical Canons”; first ed.: 1388], Yuji weiyi 玉機微義 [“Subtle Meanings of the Secrets Carved in Jade”; compiled on the basis of a shorter manuscript by Xu Yanchun 徐彥純 (d. 1384); first ed.: 1396], Zabing zhili 雜病治例 [“Examples of Treatments for Miscellaneous Illnesses”; first ed.: 1408?], and Shanghai zhili 傷寒治例 [“Examples of Treatments for Cold Injury”; first ed.: 1479]. The table is based on information found in the Quanguo Zhongyi tushu lianbe mulu 全國中醫圖書聯合目錄 (Xue Qinglu 1991), a catalog of Chinese medical books that have survived in Chinese libraries, as well as on data from prefaces to Liu's books. $ ^{2} $ In addition to four editions of the Yijing xiaoxue (all listed in the table below), Xue 1991 mentions copies of fifteen extant Ming editions of the Yuji weiyi. Twelve of them are identified below. I do not list the other three because they are undated. Two of these three undated editions, however, indicate the name of ...
